title: Open breach grace math can count weekend time against the 1h grace window

evidence.quote:
const duration = isOpen
      ? tradingSecondsInRange(startedAt, nowSeconds)
      : Number(b.durationSeconds ?? "0");
...
const critical = isOpen
      ? peakAboveCritical && nowSeconds > startedAt + grace
        ? tradingSecondsInRange(startedAt + grace, nowSeconds)
        : 0

reasoning: The chart correctly computes open-breach duration with tradingSecondsInRange, but the open critical/past-grace branch still gates on wall-clock nowSeconds > startedAt + grace and then starts counting from startedAt + grace, which is also a wall-clock offset. If a breach starts shortly before or during the FX weekend, the 1h grace can expire while markets are closed. Example: start Friday 20:30 UTC, now Sunday 23:15 UTC, critical peak. Only 45 trading minutes have elapsed, so past-grace should be 0, but this code sees now > Friday 21:30 and reports tradingSecondsInRange(Friday 21:30, Sunday 23:15) = 15 minutes past grace. That makes the chart hover and severity boundary disagree with trading-second grace semantics used elsewhere.
reproduction: Freeze Date.now to Sunday 2024-01-07 23:15 UTC, pass one open breach started Friday 2024-01-05 20:30 UTC with peakPriceDifference / entryRebalanceThreshold above DEVIATION_CRITICAL_RATIO, and inspect the Plot trace customdata: it reports nonzero Past grace even though total trading duration is still under 1h.
recommendation: For open rows, derive past-grace from trading seconds directly: compute openTradingDuration = tradingSecondsInRange(startedAt, nowSeconds), set critical to peakAboveCritical ? Math.max(0, openTradingDuration - grace) : 0, and use the same value for chart hover/classification. Avoid using startedAt + grace as a wall-clock timestamp when the interval can cross FX weekend closure.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing weekend regression test covers BreachRow rendered HTML, but the chart component is mocked in breach-history-panel.test.tsx, so none of the Plot data/customdata produced by BreachHistoryChart is asserted.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a focused BreachHistoryChart test that stubs react-plotly.js, freezes time across a weekend, renders an open critical breach with less than 1 trading hour elapsed, and asserts the emitted trace customdata Past grace value is zero. Also cover the just-over-1-trading-hour case to ensure it starts counting after trading-time grace, not wall-clock grace.
minimumFixScope: ui-dashboard/src/components/breach-history-chart.tsx open-row critical-duration calculation plus a focused component regression test

history.note: Open critical past-grace now subtracts grace from tradingSecondsInRange duration, with weekend-spanning BreachHistoryChart regressions.
